patch.value: CONTEXT: every item needs a verifiable, git-trackable audit trail. DECISION: each item has a companion history/<id>.jsonl; every mutation appends one entry with operation/author/timestamp/JSON-Patch + before/after SHA-256; the chain replays deterministically and is integrity-verifiable; lenient fallback enables history-repair of drift. CONSEQUENCES: zero-dependency, portable, content-addressable integrity; streams grow unbounded (no compaction yet, see pm-3pbq); single-writer-per-item via lock; no random-access-by-time. EVIDENCE: src/core/history/history.ts, replay.ts, drift-scan.ts; items pm-85hm/pm-pjs5/pm-kbm9.
ADR: Append-only JSONL history with SHA-256 hash chain
Append-only JSONL history with SHA-256 hash chain
ADR recorded and accepted; architectural decision documented as durable context on 2026-05-31.
Accepted ADR recorded in living map
The ADR captures a durable architecture or operating decision with context, consequences, and evidence for future agents.
The closed Decision 'ADR: Append-only JSONL history with SHA-256 hash chain' is recorded under the ADR living log and preserved as durable project context.
